creator: kib@FreeBSD.org
text:
Why is NAT-T needed for IPv6? The main (the only ?) promise of IPv6 is to get
rid of the NAT.  Then, the technique to improve NAT traversal for ipsec might
be not too useful.

On the other hand, it is a different encapsulation which could have application
by itself.  Can you please explain your use for NAT-T over IPv6?
